question: G= ❨V,E❩V={Tom, Patricia, Hope, Sandy, Amy, Marika, Jeff, Mary}E =｛｛T, ᑭ}, {T, H}, {T, S}, {T, A}, {T, 𐌑}, {J, ᑭ}, {J, Y}, {ᑭ, H},{A, H},{A, 𐌑｝｝Plot 𝓖. Assume first letters of each name correspond to their letters. M = mary and Y= marika.
response: Let’s interpret your graph clearly first.**Vertices (V):**T (Tom), P (Patricia), H (Hope), S (Sandy), A (Amy), M (Mary), Y (Marika), J (Jeff)**Edges (E):**{T,P}, {T,H}, {T,S}, {T,A}, {T,M},{J,P}, {J,Y},{P,H},{A,H}, {A,M}---## Graph structure (intuitive layout)A clean way to “plot” this is to place **T (Tom)** in the center since it has the most connections.### Connections:* **T** connects to: P, H, S, A, M* **J** connects to: P, Y* **P** connects to: T, J, H* **A** connects to: T, H, M* **H** connects to: T, P, A* **M** connects to: T, A* **S** connects to: T only* **Y** connects to: J only---## ASCII-style sketch```        Y        |        J        |        P —— H       / \   |      /   \  |     T —— A    / \     \   S   M----- ```---## How to draw it neatly (step-by-step)1. Put **T** in the center.2. Around T, place: P, H, S, A, M.3. Connect:   * T to all five: P, H, S, A, M4. Add triangle structure:   * P — H   * H — A5. Connect:   * A — M6. Add J off to the side:   * J — P   * J
